note: Interactive valuation model. Defaults are calibrated to roughly reproduce Ferrovial's ~€43bn equity value so the sliders show sensitivity, not precision. Illustrative & educational — not a target price.

assets.name: 407 ETR
assets.note: The crown jewel. Valued on the cash it pays Ferrovial (CAD 1.5bn total dividend in 2025 × 48.29%), grown at the global toll-growth rate over the concession to 2098.

assets.name: US Managed Lanes
assets.note: NTE, LBJ, NTE 35W, I-77, I-66. Dynamic congestion pricing drives revenue-per-transaction above inflation. High multiples reflect ramp-up & pricing power (no cap on I-66/I-77).

assets.name: Airports (JFK NTO + Dalaman)
assets.note: JFK New Terminal One (49%, ~€978m equity, opening phased from late-2026) + Dalaman. Bull/bear spread: Citi ~1.1x vs BofA ~2.1x book ahead of opening.

assets.name: Construction
assets.note: Low-margin captive builder of the concessions pipeline + Budimex (listed, at market) + Webber. Modest multiple reflects cyclicality; margin normalising from a 2025 peak of 4.6%.

assets.name: Energy
assets.note: Sub-scale (~€3m EBITDA), strategic optionality: US solar + future US transmission + data-centre power adjacency.

assets.name: IRB (India)
assets.note: ~19.9% of listed IRB Infrastructure Developers (at market) + ~24% of IRB Infrastructure Trust (InvIT, acquired for ~€652m).

assets.name: Net cash (holding)
assets.note: Corporate (recourse) net CASH position of ~€1.3bn — real firepower for the US pipeline.

assets.name: Holding costs / minorities
assets.note: Capitalised corporate overhead and conglomerate/holding adjustments.

dcf.note: Illustrative group FCFF DCF. A vanilla DCF tends to UNDERSHOOT Ferrovial because it cannot 'see' the 70-year 407 concession the way a SOTP/DDM does — a key reason infrastructure is valued sum-of-the-parts.
